name: 7. 고등 삽입·동격·생략 150
description: 동격의 of, 동격의 that, 콤마 동격, 관계사 생략, 접속사 that 생략, 분사구문·부사절 생략, 삽입구/삽입절 150개 (고등 수준)

words.w: The city of Seoul is busy.
The month of May is lovely.
The concept of freedom is important.
The news of his death shocked us.
words.m: [동격의 of] 그의 죽음이라는 소식이 우리를 놀라게 했다.

words.w: The idea of traveling excites me.
The fact of the matter is clear.
The year of 2020 was hard.
The feeling of joy filled her.
The village of Ganghwa is peaceful.
The island of Jeju is beautiful.
The art of cooking takes practice.
The dream of becoming an artist is hers.
words.m: [동격의 of] 예술가가 되고 싶다는 꿈이 그녀의 것이다.

words.w: The capital of Korea is Seoul.
The title of the book is catchy.
The sound of music is pleasant.
The taste of honey is sweet.
The idea of peace inspires many.
words.m: [동격의 of] 평화라는 생각이 많은 이를 고취시킨다.

words.w: The age of technology has begun.
The sight of you makes me smile.
words.m: [동격의 of] 너를 보는 것이 나를 미소 짓게 한다.

words.w: The spirit of adventure calls us.
The fact that he lied surprised me.
words.m: [동격의 that] 그가 거짓말했다는 사실이 나를 놀라게 했다. (명사 + that절)

words.w: The news that she won spread fast.
words.m: [동격의 that] 그녀가 이겼다는 소식이 빠르게 퍼졌다.

words.w: The idea that we can succeed motivates us.
words.m: [동격의 that] 성공할 수 있다는 생각이 우리를 동기 부여한다.

words.w: The rumor that he resigned is true.
The belief that hard work pays off is common.
The statement that money can't buy happiness is debatable.
words.m: [동격의 that] 돈으로 행복을 살 수 없다는 말은 논쟁적이다.

words.w: The truth that time flies is undeniable.
words.m: [동격의 that] 시간이 빠르다는 진리는 부정할 수 없다.

words.w: The evidence that he stole is clear.
The suggestion that we leave early was wise.
words.m: [동격의 that] 우리가 일찍 떠나자는 제안은 현명했다.

words.w: The possibility that it might rain worries me.
words.m: [동격의 that] 비가 올지도 모른다는 가능성이 걱정된다.

words.w: The hope that peace will come keeps us going.
words.m: [동격의 that] 평화가 올 거라는 희망이 우리를 유지한다.

words.w: The proof that he was right convinced us.
words.m: [동격의 that] 그가 맞았다는 증거가 우리를 설득했다.

words.w: The theory that time is relative fascinates me.
words.m: [동격의 that] 시간이 상대적이라는 이론이 나를 매혹시킨다.

words.w: The assumption that everyone agrees is wrong.
The conclusion that we must act is clear.
words.m: [동격의 that] 우리가 행동해야 한다는 결론은 분명하다.

words.w: The knowledge that you care comforts me.
words.m: [동격의 that] 네가 신경 쓴다는 사실이 나를 위로한다.

words.w: The realization that I was wrong hit me.
words.m: [동격의 that] 내가 틀렸다는 깨달음이 나를 강타했다.

words.w: The claim that he invented it is doubtful.
The notion that age is just a number is popular.
words.m: [동격의 that] 나이는 숫자에 불과하다는 생각이 유명하다.

words.w: The discovery that water flows here changed maps.
words.m: [동격의 that] 물이 여기 흐른다는 발견이 지도를 바꿨다.

words.w: The question whether we should go is open.
words.m: [동격의 that] 우리가 가야 할지 여부는 미결이다. (whether 동격)

words.w: The doubt that he's lying grows.
The impression that she's kind is accurate.
The report that the economy is improving is encouraging.
words.m: [동격의 that] 경제가 나아지고 있다는 보고가 고무적이다.

words.w: The reminder that life is short motivates us.
words.m: [동격의 that] 인생이 짧다는 상기가 우리를 동기 부여한다.

words.w: My friend, Tom, is here.
words.m: [콤마 동격] 내 친구 톰이 여기 있다. (명사, 명사 + 설명, 명사)

words.w: Mr. Kim, our teacher, is kind.
words.m: [콤마 동격] 우리 선생님인 김 선생님은 친절하시다.

words.w: Paris, the capital of France, is beautiful.
Einstein, a famous physicist, was a genius.
My sister, a doctor, works hard.
words.m: [콤마 동격] 의사인 내 언니는 열심히 일한다.

words.w: The Han River, Korea's longest river, flows through Seoul.
words.m: [콤마 동격] 한국에서 가장 긴 한강은 서울을 흐른다.

words.w: Shakespeare, an English playwright, wrote many plays.
words.m: [콤마 동격] 영국 극작가 셰익스피어는 많은 희곡을 썼다.

words.w: My cat, Mittens, is white.
Seoul, the capital of Korea, is huge.
John, my neighbor, is very friendly.
words.m: [콤마 동격] 내 이웃 존은 매우 친절하다.

words.w: The Beatles, a British band, are legendary.
Mount Halla, the highest peak in Korea, is beautiful.
Tom, my best friend, moved away.
words.m: [콤마 동격] 내 가장 친한 친구 톰이 이사 갔다.

words.w: Chicago, a windy city, is in Illinois.
words.m: [콤마 동격] 바람 도시인 시카고는 일리노이에 있다.

words.w: My brother, a teacher, loves his job.
words.m: [콤마 동격] 선생님인 내 형은 자기 일을 사랑한다.

words.w: Amy, my cousin, is visiting.
words.m: [콤마 동격] 내 사촌 에이미가 방문 중이다.

words.w: The Pacific, the largest ocean, is deep.
words.m: [콤마 동격] 가장 큰 바다 태평양은 깊다.

words.w: My dog, Buddy, is loyal.
Washington, D.C., the US capital, is historic.
Leonardo da Vinci, a Renaissance artist, painted Mona Lisa.
words.m: [콤마 동격] 르네상스 예술가 레오나르도 다 빈치는 모나리자를 그렸다.

words.w: The book I read was great.
words.m: [관계사 생략] 내가 읽은 책은 훌륭했다. (목적격 which/that 생략)

words.w: The man I met is kind.
words.m: [관계사 생략] 내가 만난 남자는 친절하다. (whom/that 생략)

words.w: The house we live in is new.
words.m: [관계사 생략] 우리가 사는 집은 새 것이다.

words.w: The food she made was delicious.
The movie we watched was long.
The car he bought is red.
The song I like is old.
The people I know are nice.
The question he asked was hard.
The letter she wrote is long.
The man standing there is my father.
words.m: [관계사 생략] 저기 서 있는 남자가 우리 아버지이다. (who is 생략: 주격관계사+be동사)

words.w: The girl wearing red is my sister.
words.m: [관계사 생략] 빨간 옷 입은 소녀가 내 여동생이다.

words.w: The house built last year is huge.
words.m: [관계사 생략] 작년에 지어진 집은 거대하다. (that was 생략)

words.w: The boy called Tom is smart.
The car parked here is mine.
words.m: [관계사 생략] 여기 주차된 차가 내 것이다.

words.w: The book written by her is famous.
The teacher loved by students retired.
The girl sitting next to me is Mary.
words.m: [관계사 생략] 내 옆에 앉은 소녀는 메리이다.

words.w: The pictures taken in Paris are beautiful.
The letter written last week was found.
The food left on the table is cold.
The dog playing in the yard is ours.
words.m: [관계사 생략] 마당에서 노는 개가 우리 것이다.

words.w: The guests invited to the party came.
The plan proposed by him failed.
The child lost in the mall cried.
words.m: [관계사 생략] 쇼핑몰에서 길 잃은 아이가 울었다.

words.w: I think he is right.
words.m: [접속사 that 생략] 나는 그가 맞다고 생각한다. (think (that) he is)

words.w: She said she was tired.
words.m: [접속사 that 생략] 그녀는 피곤하다고 말했다. (said (that) she)

words.w: I believe you can do it.
words.m: [접속사 that 생략] 나는 네가 할 수 있다고 믿는다.

words.w: He knew I was coming.
words.m: [접속사 that 생략] 그는 내가 오는 줄 알았다.

words.w: I hope everything goes well.
words.m: [접속사 that 생략] 나는 모든 것이 잘 되기를 바란다.

words.w: She feels she can succeed.
words.m: [접속사 that 생략] 그녀는 자기가 성공할 수 있다고 느낀다.

words.w: I guess you're right.
words.m: [접속사 that 생략] 네가 맞는 것 같다.

words.w: He assumes I agree.
words.m: [접속사 that 생략] 그는 내가 동의한다고 가정한다.

words.w: I suspect he is hiding something.
words.m: [접속사 that 생략] 나는 그가 뭔가 숨기고 있다고 의심한다.

words.w: She thinks he is kind.
words.m: [접속사 that 생략] 그녀는 그가 친절하다고 생각한다.

words.w: I know you are busy.
words.m: [접속사 that 생략] 나는 네가 바쁜 것을 안다.

words.w: He said he would come.
I feel something is wrong.
words.m: [접속사 that 생략] 뭔가 잘못됐다는 느낌이 든다.

words.w: She believes he is honest.
words.m: [접속사 that 생략] 그녀는 그가 정직하다고 믿는다.

words.w: I realize I made a mistake.
words.m: [접속사 that 생략] 나는 실수했다는 것을 깨닫는다.

words.w: When young, he lived in Seoul.
words.m: [분사구문·부사절 생략] 어릴 때 그는 서울에 살았다. (= When he was young, S+be 생략)

words.w: If possible, I'll come.
words.m: [분사구문·부사절 생략] 가능하면 올 것이다. (= If it is possible)

words.w: When angry, she yells.
Though tired, he kept working.
While studying, I heard a noise.
If necessary, call me.
When ready, please come.
Though hungry, she didn't eat.
While walking, I met him.
If born again, I'd choose the same.
words.m: [분사구문·부사절 생략] 다시 태어나도 같은 선택을 할 것이다.

words.w: When in Rome, do as the Romans do.
Though old, he is energetic.
If invited, I'll attend.
While sleeping, he snored loudly.
words.m: [분사구문·부사절 생략] 자는 동안 그는 크게 코를 골았다.

words.w: When finished, submit your paper.
If asked, I'll tell the truth.
Though poor, they are happy.
When surprised, she gasped.
words.m: [분사구문·부사절 생략] 놀랐을 때 그녀는 숨을 삼켰다.

words.w: If lost, stay calm.
While at school, study hard.
words.m: [분사구문·부사절 생략] 학교에 있는 동안 열심히 공부해라.

words.w: It is, I think, a good idea.
words.m: [삽입구/삽입절] 이것은 내 생각에 좋은 생각이다. (I think 삽입)

words.w: The answer, however, is wrong.
The book, in my opinion, is excellent.
She is, of course, my friend.
He is, as you know, very busy.
The weather, for example, changes fast.
The result, in fact, was surprising.
She, on the other hand, disagrees.
This plan, by the way, is brilliant.
He, I believe, will come.
The food, to be honest, was terrible.
My father, who is kind, helped me.
She is, needless to say, brilliant.
The movie, so to speak, is a masterpiece.
He, as a matter of fact, is my cousin.
This is, I'm afraid, impossible.
He failed, unfortunately, the test.
She was, surprisingly, on time.
The answer, it seems, is no.
He is, after all, my brother.
This, in short, is the plan.
She, believe it or not, won first place.
He, to tell the truth, was lying.
The food, more or less, tasted okay.
She is, above all, a good friend.
